survival_formula: S = (1-T) + T / (1 + lambda * D), lambda=2.0, D=distance+1

H2a.description: S_z predicts affect (LMM, random subject intercepts, within-person z-scored)

H2b.description: k_z moderates S_z -> affect: LMM with S_z + S_z:k_z interaction. k_z is between-subjects and collinear with random intercept (main effect not estimable); only S_z:k_z interaction is reported.
H2b.note_on_k_z_main_effect: k_z is a between-subjects variable. In a random-intercept LMM it is collinear with the random effect; the main effect of k_z cannot be separately estimated. Only the S_z:k_z cross-level interaction is identified and reported.

H2c.description: Cross-domain: per-subject OLS slope of choice/affect on threat. Negative choice slope = greater avoidance under threat. Positive anxiety slope = greater anxiety under threat. Negative confidence slope = greater confidence drop under threat.
H2c.note_on_signs: r(choice_slope, anxiety_slope)=-0.386: more negative choice slope (more avoidance) pairs with more positive anxiety slope (more anxiety) — coherent greater threat sensitivity. r(choice_slope, confidence_slope)=+0.372: more negative choice slope pairs with more negative confidence slope (more confidence drop) — also coherent.

choice_vs_anxiety.interpretation: Coherent cross-domain coupling: more threat-sensitive choosers are also more threat-sensitive in anxiety

choice_vs_confidence.interpretation: Coherent cross-domain coupling: more threat-sensitive choosers show larger threat-induced confidence drops
